newsSince: No new material news since June 9. The Q1 record (revenue NT$809.2B +66.6% YoY, AI servers >75% of server revenue, GB300 rack shipments doubling, triple-digit AI-server growth reiterated) was disclosed at the May 14 call; May revenue (+94% YoY) was already in the prior case. Price flat (-0.7%).

sources.label: Quanta sees triple-digit AI server growth 2026 (Investing.com)

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 16. AI-server execution (GB300 ramp, >75% server mix) is fully consensus and ODM story well-broadcast; no new info since June 9. On this crowding-sensitive page residual alpha is thinner than top names but the central-node positioning is intact. No rank-changing event.
verdicts.bull: QCT one of two largest AI rack builders; GB300 orders 'unimaginable', triple-digit AI-server growth.
verdicts.neutral: Top-line confirmed but margins thin (4.78% GM); story fully priced in.
verdicts.bear: ODM thesis is consensus; <5% gross margin and memory-cost dilution cap residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Gross-margin compression from memory costs / high-priced racks.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the deployment/systems-integration lens, May rev +94% YoY and Q1 +66.6% YoY confirm rack-integration capacity is converting to shipments, with CFO more bullish than 30 days prior. Incremental positive on deployment-bottleneck directness, not a rank-changing event.
verdicts.bull: Direct physical-deployment bottleneck exposure; rack integration converting to record shipments.
verdicts.neutral: Strong throughput but commoditized assembly margins.
verdicts.bear: Thin-margin integrator; pricing power limited vs upstream silicon bottlenecks.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued AI-server share gains / rack mix shift.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI server OEM pricing / margin pressure.
